name: Microsoft Build Engine started by Office
description: This prompt detects the execution of Microsoft Build Engine (msbuild.exe) by Microsoft Office products. Attackers can use this technique for defense evasion by using a legitimate process to execute malicious code. This activity is often an indicator of a malicious document (e.g., a macro-enabled Office file) attempting to run code on the system.

prompts.prompt: Investigate the security event where the process `{image}` was spawned by a parent process from the Microsoft Office suite (`{parent_image}`). The event occurred at `{timestamp}` on the host `{hostname}`. Analyze the command line arguments (`{command_line}`) for any suspicious parameters. Check the reputation of the user `{user}` and the context of the activity. Provide a summary of your findings and determine if this is a legitimate software execution or a potential malicious activity, and suggest mitigation steps.

parameters.name: image
parameters.description: The name of the process that was executed (should be msbuild.exe).

parameters.name: parent_image
parameters.description: The parent process that initiated the execution, which should be a Microsoft Office application (e.g., winword.exe, excel.exe).

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: The hostname where the event was detected.

parameters.name: command_line
parameters.description: The full command line of the msbuild.exe process.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account under which the process was executed.
